mapping.a_element: multi-atom ensemble storing spatial modes of light (quantum memory)
mapping.b_element: cloud of cold atoms providing gain and scattering (random laser medium)

mapping.a_element: optical parallelism enabling higher information capacity
mapping.b_element: light transport in a scattering medium enabling lasing

mapping.a_element: quantum features (multimode superposition, entanglement) stored in the ensemble
mapping.b_element: coherent light emission (lasing) arising from collective atomic interactions

brief_justification: While both papers involve light-matter interactions in atomic ensembles, paper A focuses on quantum memory via coherent storage of spatial modes, whereas paper B addresses a random laser threshold via light transport and gain in a scattering medium. The core mechanisms—quantum state preservation vs. lasing via multiple scattering—are fundamentally distinct, despite superficial overlap in using atomic ensembles and optical fields.
